name: NetworkFlow
summary: The NetworkFlow collector gathers historical data on network usage from managed devices.

fields.name: src_ip
fields.description: IP address of the source of the packet. Supports CIDR block notation.

fields.name: dst_ip
fields.description: IP address of the destination of the packet. Supports CIDR block notation.

fields.name: time
fields.description: Date and time when the packet was collected.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the TCP transaction (not available in UDP transactions). The TCP status must be interpreted as follows: On a TCP connection open operation, the CONNECTED value means that the source endpoint sent a SYN message and received an ACK,SYN message from the remote server. On a TCP connection close operation, the CLOSED value means that the source endpoint sent a SYN message and received an ACK,FIN message from the destination server. The final ACK message is ignored on both open and close operations. Important The TCP status must be interpreted as follows: On a TCP connection open operation, the CONNECTED value means that the source endpoint sent a SYN message and received an ACK,SYN message from the remote server. On a TCP connection close operation, the CLOSED value means that the source endpoint sent a SYN message and received an ACK,FIN message from the destination server. The final ACK message is ignored on both open and close operations.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that owns the originating process.

fields.name: user_id
fields.description: The user ID of the process owning the socket.

fields.name: proto
fields.description: The packet's protocol: TCP or UDP.

fields.name: direction
fields.description: Specifies whether the packet came in to the managed device, or was sent out of the device.

fields.name: ip_class
fields.description: Specifies the IP class used for the transaction: IPv4 returns 0 IPv6 returns 1 Unknown returns 2

fields.name: seq_number
fields.description: TCP transaction sequence number (not available in UDP transactions).

fields.name: dst_mac
fields.description: MAC address of destination device (Linux only).

fields.name: md5
fields.description: The MD5 hash code for the source process.

fields.name: sha1
fields.description: The SHA-1 hash code for the source process.

fields.name: sha256
fields.description: The SHA-256 hash code for the source process (Windows, macOS only).

examples.title: Example: Show process IDs and image names for network flow originating on CIDR block 10.250.45.0/24 and targeting device 10.0.0.2.
examples.v2_query: NetworkFlow process, process_id where NetworkFlow src_ip contains 10.250.45.0/24 and NetworkFlow dst_ip equals 10.0.0.2

examples.title: Connections by dest port 53
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"

examples.title: Connections by OSI protocol - single
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"
        AND NetworkFlow proto equals "tcp"

examples.title: Connections by OSI protocol - many
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"
        AND NetworkFlow proto equals "tcp"
            OR NetworkFlow proto equals "udp"

examples.title: Connections by dest port number - single
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"

examples.title: Connections by dest port number - many
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"
        OR NetworkFlow dst_port equals "443"
        OR NetworkFlow dst_port equals "80"

examples.title: Connections by dest IP and dest port 53 - single
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"
        AND NetworkFlow dst_ip equals "10.0.0.1"

examples.title: Connections by dest IP and dest port 53 - many
examples.v2_query:
NetworkFlow status, proto, direction, src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port
    WHERE NetworkFlow dst_port equals "53"
        AND NetworkFlow dst_ip equals "10.0.0.1"
            OR NetworkFlow dst_ip equals "10.0.0.2"
            OR NetworkFlow dst_ip equals "10.0.0.3"
